review-crew/audit-debt: accumulated technical, security, and architectural debt
rather than reviewing a single change
review-crew/review-code: reviewing code changes on a local branch or an open pull request
auto-fixed locally or posted to GitHub
review-crew/review-init: before the first review-crew review in a repo
regenerate or refresh a project's review calibration
review-crew/review-spec: the plain-language requirements / the *what* for a work-item
review-crew/review-tasks: bite-sized, test-first executable steps for a work-item
before the producer's Build runs it
test-pilot/test-pilot-execute: test-pilot plan should be exercised before human spot-check
Drives the app via a browser MCP
test-pilot/test-pilot-init: before the first test-pilot plan/execute in a repo
Sets up the profile, seeding blocks, and browser tooling
test-pilot/test-pilot-plan: PR/branch needs a manual test plan with seeded data
Seeds scenarios via the test-pilot engine
posts a checkbox plan to the PR
Does NOT execute the plan (that is test-pilot-execute)
the-architect/discovery: START of any new piece of work in a superheroes project
Elicits requirements (incl. significant unhappy paths) with the owner
the-architect/plan: turn it into the technical `plan` (the *how*) for a work-item
Produces the `plan` definition-doc and runs review-plan
the-architect/tasks: turn it into the bite-sized, test-first executable `tasks` for a work-item
produces the `tasks` definition-doc, then runs review-tasks
the-architect/writing-specs: author the on-disk `spec` definition-doc once an owner has APPROVED
workhorse/workhorse: approved tasks doc (gates.review == passed) should be BUILT
It NEVER merges — that is always yours
